title: Bumper Stuck
shortDescription: The front bumper sensor is jammed or continuously pressed.
fullDescription: Error 0001 on Ecovacs DEEBOT indicates the front bumper is stuck in a pressed position. The bumper must spring back freely for the robot to navigate safely. Debris in the bumper gap or a misaligned bumper causes this error.

causes.cause: Debris trapped between bumper and robot body

symptoms: Error 0001 displayed in ECOVACS HOME app
Bumper does not bounce back when pressed

fixSteps.title: Tap the bumper firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Tap the front bumper at multiple points around its perimeter to dislodge trapped debris. Push and release it several times.

fixSteps.title: Clean the bumper gap
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air to blow debris from the gap between the bumper and body. Wipe with a damp cloth to remove sticky residue.

fixSteps.title: Test bumper freedom
fixSteps.instruction: Press the bumper at multiple points. It should spring back quickly and evenly at all positions.

fixSteps.title: Reboot the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 3 seconds to turn off, then turn back on to clear the sensor state.

whenToCallPro: If the bumper microswitch has failed internally.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0001 bumper stuck fix

faq.q: Why does my DEEBOT bumper keep getting stuck?
faq.a: Dust and fine debris accumulate in the bumper gap during normal cleaning. Clean the gap weekly.

faq.q: Can a stuck bumper damage the robot?
faq.a: No, but it prevents navigation and stops the cleaning cycle.

faq.q: How do I prevent bumper jams?
faq.a: Regularly clean the gap between the bumper and body with compressed air.

title: Driving Wheel Malfunction
shortDescription: One or both driving wheels are stuck, suspended, or not functioning.
fullDescription: Error 0002 indicates a drive wheel problem. A wheel may be jammed by debris, suspended in the air, or the motor is not responding. The robot cannot navigate without both wheels operating properly.

causes.cause: Hair or debris wrapped around wheel axle
Robot stuck on a ledge with wheel suspended

symptoms: One or both wheels not spinning
Wheel does not spring back when pushed

fixSteps.title: Free the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on a flat surface. Check both wheels for debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean wheel wells
fixSteps.instruction: Remove hair and debris from both wheel wells. Push each wheel up and down to test the spring mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel operation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin each wheel by hand. Both should rotate freely with minimal resistance.

fixSteps.title: Replace wheel module if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If a wheel motor has failed, replace the entire wheel module. This involves removing the bottom plate and disconnecting the module.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel motor has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0002 wheel stuck fix

faq.q: How do I know which wheel is faulty?
faq.a: Flip the robot and spin each wheel. The faulty one will feel stiff or not rotate.

faq.q: Can I lubricate the wheels?
faq.a: A small amount of silicone lubricant on the axle is acceptable. Avoid petroleum-based oils.

faq.q: Why does my DEEBOT spin in circles?
faq.a: One wheel is not functioning properly. Clean or replace the affected wheel module.

title: Cliff Sensor Error
shortDescription: The cliff sensors are dirty or detecting a persistent drop-off.
fullDescription: Error 0003 means the cliff sensors are either obstructed by dust or detecting that the robot is near a ledge. The sensors prevent the robot from falling down stairs.

causes.cause: Robot near a stairway or ledge

fixSteps.title: Move to safe location
fixSteps.instruction: Place the robot on a flat, light-colored floor away from stairs.

fixSteps.title: Clean cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe all cliff sensor windows on the underside of the robot with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Blow out debris
fixSteps.instruction: Use compressed air around the cliff sensor areas to remove fine dust.

fixSteps.title: Set no-go zones for dark floors
fixSteps.instruction: If dark floors cause persistent issues, set no-go zones in the ECOVACS HOME app.

whenToCallPro: If sensors remain unresponsive after thorough cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0003 cliff sensor fix

faq.q: Why does my DEEBOT avoid black rugs?
faq.a: The IR cliff sensors may interpret very dark surfaces as drop-offs. This is a known limitation.

faq.q: How often should I clean cliff sensors?
faq.a: Weekly, as part of routine maintenance.

faq.q: Can I disable cliff sensors?
faq.a: Ecovacs does not provide an official option to disable cliff sensors for safety reasons.

title: Suction Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The vacuum suction motor is stalled or not reaching proper speed.
fullDescription: Error 0004 indicates the vacuum fan motor cannot operate at the required speed, typically due to a clogged filter, full dustbin, or blocked suction pathway.

causes.cause: Suction path blocked by large debris

symptoms: Robot feels warm near the dustbin

fixSteps.title: Empty the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the dustbin completely. Tap it to dislodge compacted dust.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter, tap off dust, and rinse under water if washable. Air dry for 24 hours.

fixSteps.title: Check suction pathway
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the intake port and internal pathway for large debris blockages.

fixSteps.title: Replace filter if worn
fixSteps.instruction: If the filter is damaged or excessively discolored after cleaning, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0004 suction fan filter fix

faq.q: How often should I replace the DEEBOT filter?
faq.a: Every 2-3 months, or sooner if suction is noticeably reduced.

faq.q: Can I wash the DEEBOT filter?
faq.a: Most DEEBOT filters are washable. Rinse and air dry for 24 hours before reinstalling.

faq.q: Why does Error 0004 appear right after emptying the bin?
faq.a: The filter is likely clogged. Even with an empty bin, a dirty filter restricts airflow.

title: Main Brush Stalled
shortDescription: The main brush roller is jammed and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error 0005 indicates the main brush is stalled by tangled hair, fibers, or debris preventing rotation.

causes.cause: Hair tangled around the main brush

fixSteps.title: Remove the main brush
fixSteps.instruction: Flip the robot over, release the brush guard, and pull out the main brush.

fixSteps.title: Remove tangled debris
fixSteps.instruction: Cut and remove all hair, string, and fibers. Clean the end caps and bearings.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brush housing
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the brush cavity with a dry cloth to remove packed debris.

fixSteps.title: Replace if worn
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main brush if bristles are worn flat or rubber parts are torn.

whenToCallPro: If the brush motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0005 main brush stuck fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the DEEBOT main brush?
faq.a: Weekly, or more often with pets.

faq.q: Can the main brush be washed?
faq.a: Yes, rinse under water and dry completely before reinstalling.

faq.q: Does the X2 Omni have an anti-tangle brush?
faq.a: Yes, the X2 Omni features a rubber brush designed to resist hair tangling.

title: Side Brush Stalled
shortDescription: The side brush is obstructed and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: Error 0006 means the side brush is jammed, typically by hair or string wrapped around the axle.

causes.cause: Hair wrapped around side brush axle

symptoms: Clicking noise from side brush area

fixSteps.title: Remove the side brush
fixSteps.instruction: Unscrew the side brush screw and pull off the brush.

fixSteps.title: Clean the axle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove hair and debris from the axle post.

fixSteps.title: Test rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the axle by hand to check for smooth movement.

fixSteps.title: Replace if damaged
fixSteps.instruction: Install a new side brush if the bristles are bent or the mounting hole is stripped.

whenToCallPro: If the side brush motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0006 side brush fix

faq.q: How often should I replace the side brush?
faq.a: Every 3-6 months depending on usage.

faq.q: Does DEEBOT have one or two side brushes?
faq.a: Most models have one. The X2 Omni has two side brushes.

faq.q: Can I use the robot without the side brush?
faq.a: Yes, but edge cleaning will be poor.

title: Navigation Sensor Error
shortDescription: The LDS or TrueMapping navigation sensor is not functioning properly.
fullDescription: Error 0007 indicates the LIDAR or TrueMapping navigation sensor cannot operate correctly. The sensor turret may be stuck, dirty, or the sensor has a hardware fault.
symptoms: Robot navigation is random instead of systematic

fixSteps.title: Free the LDS turret
fixSteps.instruction: Gently spin the LDS turret with your finger. It should rotate freely. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Clean the LDS window
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the transparent window around the turret with a dry microfiber cloth.

fixSteps.title: Remove hair from turret base
fixSteps.instruction: Check the base of the LDS turret for tangled hair and remove it.

fixSteps.title: Reboot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 3 seconds to restart and recalibrate the sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the LDS motor or sensor has physically failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0007 LDS navigation sensor fix

faq.q: What is TrueMapping?
faq.a: TrueMapping is Ecovacs' LIDAR-based navigation system that creates precise maps of your home.

faq.q: Does the X2 Omni use LIDAR?
faq.a: The X2 Omni uses a combination of LIDAR and cameras for TrueMapping 2.0 navigation.

faq.q: Can I manually spin the LDS turret?
faq.a: Yes, it should rotate with very light force. If stuck, clean the base area.

title: Dustbin Not Detected
shortDescription: The dustbin is missing, not properly installed, or the detection sensor is faulty.
fullDescription: Error 0008 occurs when the robot cannot detect the dustbin. The bin must be properly seated for the suction pathway to function.
symptoms: Dustbin appears seated but not recognized

fixSteps.title: Reseat the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and firmly reinsert the dustbin until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean detection contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the contacts on the dustbin and robot with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Inspect latch tabs
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the latch mechanism is intact and not cracked.

fixSteps.title: Try a replacement bin
fixSteps.instruction: If available, test with a different dustbin to isolate the problem.

whenToCallPro: If the bin detection sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0008 dustbin not detected fix

faq.q: Can I run DEEBOT without a dustbin?
faq.a: No, the dustbin must be detected for the robot to operate.

faq.q: Why does my dustbin keep disconnecting?
faq.a: The latch may be worn. Check for damage and replace the bin if needed.

faq.q: Does the auto-empty station affect this?
faq.a: No, the robot's internal bin detection is separate from the station.

title: Water Tank Error
shortDescription: The mopping water tank is not detected, empty, or malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error 0009 applies to DEEBOT models with mopping capability. The water tank is missing, empty, or the tank sensor cannot detect it.
symptoms: Robot refuses to start mop mode

fixSteps.title: Refill and reseat tank
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the water tank, fill it with clean water, and reinsert until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Check the tank seal
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rubber gasket for damage or debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean detection area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the tank contact area on the robot.

fixSteps.title: Switch to vacuum-only
fixSteps.instruction: If the tank issue persists, use vacuum-only mode while troubleshooting.

whenToCallPro: If the tank detection sensor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0009 water tank fix mopping

faq.q: What water should I use?
faq.a: Clean tap water only. Do not add detergent or cleaning solutions to the tank.

faq.q: How often should I refill?
faq.a: Before each mopping session for best results.

faq.q: Can I mop without the water tank?
faq.a: No, the mopping function requires a properly installed water tank.

title: Mop Pad or OZMO System Error
shortDescription: The mopping pad or OZMO mopping system is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: Error 0010 indicates an issue with the mopping system, including the mop pad attachment, the OZMO vibrating mechanism, or the water delivery system.
symptoms: Mop does not vibrate during mopping
Mop pad falls off during cleaning

fixSteps.title: Reattach mop pad
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and firmly reattach the mop pad to the mopping plate.

fixSteps.title: Clean the mopping plate
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the mopping plate and pad attachment area clean.

fixSteps.title: Check water flow
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water tank is full and the delivery holes are not clogged.

fixSteps.title: Reboot
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the robot to reinitialize the mopping module.

whenToCallPro: If the OZMO vibration motor has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0010 mop OZMO fix

faq.q: What is OZMO?
faq.a: OZMO is Ecovacs' smart mopping technology that precisely controls water flow for effective mopping.

faq.q: How often should I replace mop pads?
faq.a: Wash after each use. Replace every 2-3 months or when worn.

faq.q: Can I use disposable mop pads?
faq.a: Yes, Ecovacs offers disposable mop pad options for convenience.

title: Cannot Locate Charging Dock
shortDescription: The robot cannot find its way back to the charging dock.
fullDescription: Error 0011 means the robot has been unable to locate and return to its charging dock. The dock signal may be blocked, the dock moved, or the robot is too far away.

causes.cause: Objects blocking the dock IR signal
Robot in unmapped area far from dock

symptoms: Robot stops with low battery in random location

fixSteps.title: Check dock placement
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dock is against a wall with 50cm clear space on each side and 1.5m in front.

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove objects near the dock that could block the IR signal.

fixSteps.title: Manually dock
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on the dock to charge.

fixSteps.title: Update dock position
fixSteps.instruction: If the dock was moved, update the map in the ECOVACS HOME app.

whenToCallPro: Only if the dock hardware is failing.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0011 cannot find dock fix

faq.q: Where should I place the DEEBOT dock?
faq.a: On a flat surface against a wall with clear space around it. Avoid corners and glass walls.

faq.q: Can sunlight interfere with docking?
faq.a: Yes, direct sunlight can disrupt the IR signal used for docking.

faq.q: What if the dock was moved?
faq.a: The robot needs to remap or update its dock position in the app.

title: Low Battery
shortDescription: The battery is too low to start or continue cleaning.
fullDescription: Error 0012 appears when the battery has insufficient charge to begin or continue a cleaning mission.

fixSteps.title: Dock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Place the robot on the charging dock and allow a full charge (3-4 hours).

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the contacts on the robot and dock with a dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check dock power
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dock is plugged into a working outlet.

fixSteps.title: Replace battery if old
fixSteps.instruction: If runtime has significantly decreased, the battery needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the battery is swollen or will not accept charge.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0012 low battery fix replacement

faq.q: How long should DEEBOT run on a full charge?
faq.a: Most models run 110-180 minutes depending on the model and suction level.

faq.q: Does DEEBOT support resume-after-charge?
faq.a: Yes, most models will return to the dock, charge, and resume cleaning.

faq.q: How long does the battery last?
faq.a: Typically 2-4 years with normal use.

title: Charging Error
shortDescription: The robot is unable to charge properly on the dock.
fullDescription: Error 0013 indicates a charging failure. The robot is docked but not receiving power or the charging circuit is malfunctioning.
symptoms: Robot on dock but not charging
Battery does not increase over time

fixSteps.title: Clean contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe charging contacts on robot and dock with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Redock carefully
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and redock the robot ensuring proper contact alignment.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the dock adapter is plugged in and the outlet works. Try a different outlet.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle dock
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dock for 60 seconds and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the charging circuit has failed internally.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0013 charging dock fix

faq.q: Why does my DEEBOT not charge on the dock?
faq.a: The most common cause is dirty charging contacts. Clean them regularly.

faq.q: Can I use a different power adapter?
faq.a: Only use the adapter provided by Ecovacs to avoid damage.

faq.q: How do I know if charging is working?
faq.a: The LED indicator on the robot or dock should show the charging status.

title: Map Data Error
shortDescription: The stored map data is corrupted or cannot be loaded.
fullDescription: Error 0014 indicates that the robot's stored map is corrupted, cannot be loaded, or the environment has changed too much for the robot to localize itself.
symptoms: Map blank or corrupted in app

fixSteps.title: Delete and remap
fixSteps.instruction: Delete the current map in the ECOVACS HOME app and let the robot create a new one.

fixSteps.title: Remove unused maps
fixSteps.instruction: Delete any unused floor maps to free storage.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Install any available firmware updates from the app.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset
fixSteps.instruction: As a last resort, factory reset through the app.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed unless internal storage has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT error 0014 map corrupted remap fix

faq.q: How many maps can DEEBOT save?
faq.a: Most models support 2-4 maps for multi-floor homes.

faq.q: Will remapping lose my room labels?
faq.a: Yes, you will need to re-label rooms and set up zones again.

faq.q: How long does remapping take?
faq.a: One to two cleaning runs to create a complete map.

title: Docking Station Communication Error
shortDescription: The robot cannot communicate with the OMNI or auto-empty station.
fullDescription: Error 0015 applies to models with advanced docking stations (OMNI stations). The robot has docked but cannot establish communication with the station for auto-empty, mop washing, or other functions.

fixSteps.title: Clean station contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe all contacts on the robot and station with isopropyl alcohol.

fixSteps.title: Redock the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and carefully redock the robot on the station.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle station
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the station for 60 seconds and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Update both robot and station firmware through the ECOVACS HOME app.

whenToCallPro: If the station communication board has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Ecovacs DEEBOT OMNI station error 0015 communication fix

faq.q: What functions does the OMNI station provide?
faq.a: Auto-empty dustbin, auto mop washing and drying, auto water refill, and auto solution dispensing.

faq.q: Can I use the robot without the OMNI station?
faq.a: Yes, with a basic charging dock, but you lose the automatic maintenance features.

faq.q: How do I update the station firmware?
faq.a: Updates are delivered through the ECOVACS HOME app when the robot is docked.
